title: Personal Space ATFs Bathing Instrumentality
wikitext:
[]: Personal Space, ATFs, Bathing, Instrumentality             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jul 20, 2014 1:04 am Just gonna get right into this one, so here we go: As early as episode 02, the bath is used as a symbol of washing away one's worries and guilt. Misato suggests to Shinji to take a bath, claiming that it cleans the mind and the soul. However, while in the bath Shinji expresses that bad memories always seem to find him there. In a sense, the bathtub is a personal space wherein a person can lay in introspection and reflect upon their thoughts in peace, laying naked and exposed without worry. For Shinji his negative thoughts catch up with him, but for Misato this seems to have a much more therapeutic result. While never again dealt with directly, this theme of the bath plays a huge role throughout the series. For Asuka, during her failed suicide, whether it be via the roman bath or starvation. Or for Shinji during his encounter with Kaworu. There's also the water drop motif that occurs during switches between reality and dream/Instrumentality. Rising above the surface suggests returning to reality while being submerged suggests Instrumentality. In EoE, the fact that lifeforms are reduced to puddles and then have to reemerge themselves from the pool in order to reform their identity also plays to this theme. The LCL that the pilots are submerged in during their synchronization with the Eva also reflects Instrumentality and the shifting of ego to an extent. Could this play into similar religious themes of baptism? What are your thoughts on this theme throughout the series?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Jul 20, 2014 1:17 am Immersion gets intertwined with return to the womb and (re-)birth imagery of a more conventional sort, as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Sun Jul 20, 2014 1:36 am The bath as a place of introspection is a pretty common motif in the show. The most notable example for me is DC Episode 22, where Asuka starts breaking down and crying while thinking about sharing the tub

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sun Jul 20, 2014 11:36 am The ocean is the collective unconscious, the bath the individual unconscious. What is a bath, after all, but a little piece of the ocean?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Jul 20, 2014 6:22 pm Let it be said that different people just respond differently to baths. There are people who unfathomably prefer showers, not just for time reasons. Even as infants, people are inherently different: A common tipp for new moms is to figure out wether your new infant is invigorated or calmed by bathing, which you can then use to plan your baby's day. If bathing relaxes the baby, you'd obviously do it before trying to make it sleep etc. Shinji shows vaguely hydrophobic tendencies later in the show (He never learned to swim tc.), probably just doesn't like water. Neither does he really take to noise or heights, or new places, so yeah. As for the Asuka situation, a bathroom is also where you pretty yourself up, craft your image... Asuka reached the point where her image collapsed and she just didn't bother anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 21, 2014 12:53 pm Kendrix wrote: Shinji shows vaguely hydrophobic tendencies later in the show (He never learned to swim tc.), probably just doesn't like water. Neither does he really take to noise or heights, or new places, so yeah.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jul 21, 2014 1:58 pm Kendrix wrote: There are people who unfathomably prefer showers, not just for time reasons.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 21, 2014 2:11 pm Though OTOH expecting a foreigner to adhere to Japanese convention in all things is a tad unreasonable. I realize Asuka doesn't have much room to argue on that point, but even so.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jul 21, 2014 2:30 pm she's certainly been there long enough to know better at that point, and her rant about sharing water, air, space and utilities with them makes it clear this was a deliberate act.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 21, 2014 2:36 pm NemZ wrote: she's certainly been there long enough to know better at that point, and her rant about sharing water, air, space and utilities with them makes it clear this was a deliberate act.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Jul 21, 2014 3:42 pm Bagheera wrote: Oh, I'm sure she's aware of the custom, but think about it from her perspective: she's routinely stuck with cold, dirty water just because she's a girl? Fuck that noise.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 21, 2014 4:35 pm NemZ wrote: Typically such baths remain heated at a constant temperature, and the whole washing before you bathe thing is specifically to keep the water from being dirty.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jul 21, 2014 5:03 pm Tradition, and protocol!

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jul 21, 2014 5:07 pm pwhodges wrote: Tradition, and protocol!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 22, 2014 3:13 am Bagheera wrote: A headstrong girl from a progressive country is supposed to pay attention to that ?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Jul 22, 2014 3:34 am Sachi wrote: It's still an active disrespect for the household for wasting the water. In a post-2I society I would imagine that clean and usable water would be more scarce and thus more of a luxury to afford.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Jul 22, 2014 3:38 am Bagheera wrote: And again, she might do that on principle normally. Given her state of mind at the time, well . . .

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jul 22, 2014 1:22 pm I get the feeling this might be moot. Would Misato even really care about who goes first? She doesn't seem like the type who'd mind so long as everyone got a turn.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jul 22, 2014 1:32 pm Given the rest of her speech I don't think it's the going last thing that bugs her so much as just sharing in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Jul 23, 2014 8:22 am Sachi wrote: It's interesting that he chooses to attempt drowning himself at the beginning of EoE. Shinji rejects reality and attempts to submerge into the dream eternally;
